mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of future earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33 as of 2025-05-01 vintage, plateaued after 2023-2024 hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of five rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2-4.3 percent as of 2025-05-01 vintage, near multi-year lows but with upward drift risk
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return following triggered UNRATE breaches is not significantly more negative than unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or trigger fires fewer than 15 times in the historical sample making inference unreliable.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand strength that jointly signals durable economic momentum; neither series alone is sufficient, but their joint positive momentum produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month because it confirms demand breadth rather than a single noisy sector.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 724131 million and HOUST at 1361-1400 thousand units per 2025 vintages
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST momentum signal underperforms either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 2-year window, invalidating the interaction premise.

mechanism_narrative: A rising 1-month trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving manufacturing throughput and supply-side capacity utilization, which feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets incrementally price in stronger production data releases.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 103.88 vs revised 101.13 showing meaningful revision risk
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change falls below Rank IC of 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or sign flips persist across successive windows.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained positive payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals labor market tightness that raises wage-cost pressure and forward inflation risk, which in this regime is associated with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets anticipate tighter monetary policy responses; in early-cycle recoveries the same payroll growth instead signals demand recovery and produces positive returns, making the direction regime-dependent.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value 158498 thousand vs vintage 159561 indicating downward revision risk
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: Negative directional relationship fails to hold (60d forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline, p > 0.05) when restricted to late-cycle high-rate subsample identified via FEDFUNDS > 4%.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate level and the year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL approximates the real policy rate; when FEDFUNDS remains elevated while CPI inflation decelerates, the real rate rises mechanically, tightening financial conditions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, an effect not captured by either series individually.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33 percent while CPIAUCSL near 320.6 index level implying moderating but still positive inflation
failure_condition: Constructed real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY) shows Rank IC with forward 20d returns below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus FEDFUNDS alone in nested model comparison.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% signals an emerging contraction in residential investment, a leading indicator of broader demand-side recession risk, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised to 1400 thousand units from vintage 1361, showing volatility in this leading indicator
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following triggered HOUST YoY declines exceeding 10% are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline mean (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening regimes with FEDFUNDS above 4%, a simultaneous slowdown in industrial production growth and rising unemployment jointly signal supply-side capacity contraction combined with labor-market slack, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week as markets react quickly to confirmatory weak data prints in a high-rate environment; outside tightening regimes the same combination has muted predictive power.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4% and INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0 and UNRATE rising
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.33 percent with UNRATE at 4.2-4.3 percent as of 2025 vintages, consistent with a restrictive-hold regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy rate
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during joint-trigger episodes within high-rate regime are not significantly more negative than the unconditional 5-day mean (p > 0.05), or fewer than 10 qualifying episodes exist for reliable inference.
